title: Multi-Environment Testing
description: The checkout form needs to be verified across Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet environments. Most things work across all three. But two environment-specific bugs exist — one on Mobile, one on Tablet. The student must switch environments, test each one, document which bugs appear where, and justify their prioritisation.
context: The core checkout bugs from scene_1 have been resolved. Dan's fix for the regression from scene_2 is also deployed. The build is now being prepped for release across all device environments. The student's job is to run environment-specific testing using the Desktop/Mobile/Tablet toggle in the TestExecutionTab.

bugs.title: Card number field overflows container on mobile — last 4 digits cut off
bugs.description: On a 375px mobile viewport the card number input field does not resize correctly. The field width is fixed in pixels rather than fluid. When a 16-digit card number is entered, the last 4 digits are cut off visually — the user cannot see what they typed.
bugs.expected: Card number field should be fully visible and scrollable on mobile viewports. User should be able to see all 16 digits they entered.
bugs.actual: Last 4 digits of the card number are hidden behind the right edge of the container. User cannot confirm what they typed.
bugs.severity_justification: Mobile accounts for a significant portion of checkout traffic. A user who can't see their card number cannot verify their entry, leading to failed payments and drop-off.
bugs.spec_reference: Implied — checkout form must be usable across device types

bugs.title: Submit button partially hidden below viewport fold on tablet
bugs.description: On a 768x1024 tablet viewport the checkout form is taller than the visible area. The Submit button sits below the viewport fold. There is no scroll indicator or visual cue that the page is scrollable. Users are not likely to scroll down and will abandon the checkout.
bugs.expected: Submit button should be visible within the initial viewport without scrolling, or a clear scroll indicator should be present.
bugs.actual: Submit button is below the fold with no scroll indicator. Page is scrollable but nothing indicates this to the user.
bugs.severity_justification: Users who cannot see the Submit button cannot complete checkout. Tablet users represent a secondary but meaningful traffic segment.
bugs.spec_reference: Implied — checkout form must be completable across device types

bp_sqa_5.description: Student reports environment-specific bugs and provides a prioritisation with reasoning

attention_to_detail.high: Student tested all available environments (per their difficulty level), identified all environment-specific bugs, and correctly tagged each bug with the environment it appears in
attention_to_detail.medium: Student found one of the two environment bugs but missed the other, or found both but didn't tag them with the correct environment
attention_to_detail.low: Student only tested Desktop, missed both environment-specific bugs, or submitted a report saying all environments are clean

analytical_reasoning.high: Prioritises Mobile bug (BUG-ENV-001) above Tablet bug (BUG-ENV-002) with a specific reason — references mobile traffic share, checkout conversion impact, or the fact that card number invisibility directly causes failed payments (not just abandonment)
analytical_reasoning.medium: Prioritises Mobile above Tablet but reasoning is generic ('mobile has more users') without connecting to specific business impact
analytical_reasoning.low: Treats both bugs as equally important with no prioritisation reasoning, or prioritises Tablet over Mobile with no justification

decisiveness.high: Makes a clear prioritisation decision and states it explicitly — 'I am marking BUG-ENV-001 as higher priority because...'
decisiveness.medium: Suggests Mobile is probably more important but doesn't commit to an explicit priority order
decisiveness.low: Defers the prioritisation decision — 'both are important, the PM should decide'

good_signals: tests Mobile environment and finds the card field overflow bug
tests Tablet environment and finds the hidden Submit button bug
tags each bug report with the specific environment it occurs in
prioritises Mobile bug over Tablet bug with reasoning
references mobile traffic share or checkout conversion impact as the reason for prioritisation
notes that Desktop environment is clean
bad_signals: only tests Desktop — doesn't switch environments at all
finds both bugs but treats them as equally important with no prioritisation
misses the Mobile card overflow bug
misses the Tablet Submit button bug

easy.hint_text: Tip: On Desktop the checkout looks fine. The issues appear on different screen sizes. Try switching environments using the toggle buttons.
easy.note: Easy mode only shows Desktop — student is introduced to the concept of environment testing but not yet asked to compare environments

medium.hint_text: Check how the form renders on mobile. Some issues only appear at smaller screen sizes.

hard.dan_pushback_on_tablet_priority: If you're saying Mobile is higher priority than Tablet, I need a reason. My manager is asking which one to fix first.
